title: Callista coelogyne
intro: Callista coelogyne, also known as Coelogyne pandurata, is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne pandurata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coelogyne pandurata is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, as well as being used in the production of tinctures and extracts.
